Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
He spoke of me as united in faith with him; but what is his faith? Why are his books kept secret? I can meet any attack.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: In the meantime, I desired to free myself from suspicion in the implicit judgment of the reader, and to refute the gravest of the charges in the eyes of my friends.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I did not wish it to appear that I had been the first to strike, seeing that I have not, even when wounded, aimed a blow against my assailant, but have only sought to heal my own wound.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I beg the reader to let the blame rest on him who struck the first blow, without respect of persons.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He is not content with striking; but, as if he were dealing with a man whom he had reduced to silence and who would never speak again, he has written three elaborate books and has made out from my works a list of “Contradictions” worthy of Marcion.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Our minds are all on fire to know at once what his doctrine is and what is this madness of mine which we had not expected.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Perhaps he has learnt (though the time for it has been short) all that is necessary to make him my teacher, and a sudden flow of eloquence will reveal what no one imagined that he knew.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: “Grant it, O Father; mighty Jesus, grant.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Let him begin the engagement hand to hand.”

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Though he may brandish the spear of his accusations and hurl them against us with all his might, we trust in the Lord our Saviour that his truth will encompass us as with a shield, and we shall be able to sing with the Psalmist: “Their blows have become as the arrows of the little ones,” and “Though an host should encamp against me, my heart shall not fear; though war should rise against me, even then will I be confident.”

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: But of this at another time.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Let us now return to the point where we began.
